subject: Re: To Lesley Re Institute Board Mtg; 2 pdf attachmnts
body:
if i'm in town

----- Original Message ----
From: Lesley Groff <lesley@nysgllc.com>
To: J. Epstein <jeeproject@yahoo.com>
Sent: Tuesday, October 24, 2006 8:43:20 AM
Subject: FW: To Lesley Re Institute Board Mtg; 2 pdf attachmnts

To Lesley Re Institute Board Mtg; 2 pdf attachmnts

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Lesley Groff

Executive Assistant to Jeffrey 
Epstein

 

 




From: Tramo, Mark Jude 
[mailto:mark_tramo@hms.harvard.edu] 
Sent: Tuesday, October 24, 2006 
6:57 AM
To: Lesley Groff
Subject: To Lesley Re Institute 
Board Mtg; 2 pdf attachmnts





Hi Lesley!

Any feedback from Mr E after my last phone 
call?

I'm writing to invite Jeffrey and you to our Institute's Board 
meeting/gala at the Faculty Club and Fogg Art Museum at Harvard on Monday Nov 
6th - the info is attached in this pdf draft that is being circulated among our 
Board members. 

Attending the Board mtg would provide JE an inside 
look into our Strategic Plan (though not commit him to joining the Board).  
The other 3 events are fun-filled - I think he would especially enjoy hearing 
and meeting Dr Krause:  Bernie has done everything from play session guitar 
for Berry Gordy in the early Motown days to replace Pete Seeger in the Weavers 
to teach Brian Wilson how to use a Moog to record with George Harrison to score 
over 100 Hollywood films (e.g., Apocalypse Now, Love Story) to get a 
Bioacoustics PhD in his 40s, whereafter he built the world's largest archive of 
natural soundscapes - about 10-20% of which are now 
extinct! 

Bernie's Program in Acoustic Ecology & Music Culture 
is  one of our 5 research divisions: Program in Auditory Neuroscience 
(Siemens invited an Executive Summary this yr re: creating an AudNSci 
Center);  Program in Learning & Edutainment (Exec Summ of proposal to 
revolutionize Tx of dyslexia in hands of Disney-Pixar thanks to Siemens 
President);  Program in Health & Medicine (Center proposal going to 
Paul Allen via his oncologist in Seattle whose cancer ctr is part of a 
multi-center clinical trial);  & Program in Technology R&D, which 
inegrates/develops all the Programs' R&D work with potential for-profit 
spinoffs for the music, audio, hospital-bed, and other industries.

I'm 
also attaching a Society for Neuroscience Press Release of new abstract out of 
the Health & Medicine Program - out of 14,000+ abstracts published for this 
month's annual meeting, the SFN's Education Director selected only 700 for 
inclusion heir Press Book.  (This one may be of particular interest to new 
moms!)

Will call later today 
(Tues). 

Best,
Mark

Mark Jude Tramo, MD, PhD
Director, 
The Institute for Music & Brain Science
Dept of Neurology, Harvard 
Medical School & Massachusetts General Hospital
Steering Committee, 
Harvard University Mind/Brain/Behavior Interfaculty Initiative
Best Doctors 
in America®/Best of Boston(Neurology)
Writer Member, ASCAP

Mailing 
Address:  175 Cambridge St, Suite 340, Boston, MA 02114 USA
Email  
MTramo@HMS.Harvard.edu
URL  http://www.BrainMusic.org
Office Phone 
617-726-5409
Patient Phone  617-724-3992
Cellphone (24/7)  
781-718-6390
Fax 781-990-1397
Page Operator (24/7)  
617-726-2066)

END

Hi Lesley!

Any feedback from Mr E after my last phone call?

I'm writing to invite Jeffrey and you to our Institute's Board meeting/gala at the Faculty Club and Fogg Art Museum at Harvard on Monday Nov 6th - the info is attached in this pdf draft that is being circulated among our Board members. 

Attending the Board mtg would provide JE an inside look into our Strategic Plan (though not commit him to joining the Board).  The other 3 events are fun-filled - I think he would especially enjoy hearing and meeting Dr Krause:  Bernie has done everything from play session guitar for Berry Gordy in the early Motown days to replace Pete Seeger in the Weavers to teach Brian Wilson how to use a Moog to record with George Harrison to score over 100 Hollywood films (e.g., Apocalypse Now, Love Story) to get a Bioacoustics PhD in his 40s, whereafter he built the world's largest archive of natural soundscapes - about 10-20% of which are now extinct! 

Bernie's Program in Acoustic Ecology & Music Culture is  one of our 5 research divisions: Program in Auditory Neuroscience (Siemens invited an Executive Summary this yr re: creating an AudNSci Center);  Program in Learning & Edutainment (Exec Summ of proposal to revolutionize Tx of dyslexia in hands of Disney-Pixar thanks to Siemens President);  Program in Health & Medicine (Center proposal going to Paul Allen via his oncologist in Seattle whose cancer ctr is part of a multi-center clinical trial);  & Program in Technology R&D, which inegrates/develops all the Programs' R&D work with potential for-profit spinoffs for the music, audio, hospital-bed, and other industries.

I'm also attaching a Society for Neuroscience Press Release of new abstract out of the Health & Medicine Program - out of 14,000+ abstracts published for this month's annual meeting, the SFN's Education Director selected only 700 for inclusion heir Press Book.  (This one may be of particular interest to new moms!)

Will call later today (Tues).

Mark
